sourceMovie.title: Disturbia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Disturbia (2007) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Primal Fear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Disturbia clicked for you, Primal Fear (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Pacific Heights
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Disturbia clicked for you, Pacific Heights (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Disturbia?
faq.answer: Try Primal Fear, Pacific Heights, The Silence of the Lambs—each shares DNA with Disturbia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Disturbia?
faq.answer: Disturbia (2007) is best described as Thriller, Drama, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Disturbia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
